title: podman/networking issues after systemd-udev update
body:
### Issue Description

Dear Team,
I am having an issues with OEL9 and podman. If there is an systemd-udev update / reinstall, then not reachable from out of the  host all of the running containers.
Solution is if I reload the container's network or re-create the container again.
Only works if I start container with network mode host.
Thanks, Zsolt

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create network with podman
2. Create / start container with created network
3. Run telnet into container
4. run rpm -Uvh --force systemd-udev**** (reinstall your existing package)
5. run again telnet, does not work.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
version:
  APIVersion: 5.6.0
  Built: 1755868581
  BuiltTime: Fri Aug 22 13:16:21 2025
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.24.6 (Red Hat 1.24.6-1.el9)
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.6.0
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Container is running on port 9121

```
[root@test-1 user]# podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS         PORTS                   NAMES
08d433cd1c2d  localhost/cache-exporter:1.77.0  /usr/bin/cache_ex...  52 seconds ago  Up 53 seconds  0.0.0.0:9121->9121/tcp  cache-exporter
```
Telnet from another host, it works

```
[root@aaaa zkarman]# telnet 1.1.1.1 9121
Trying 1.1.1.1 ...
Connected to 1.1.1.1.
Escape character is '^]'.
```

Run systemd-udev reinstall

```
[root@test-1 user]# rpm -Uvh --force ssystemd-udev-252-51.0.4.el9_6.1.x86_64.rpm
Verifying...                          ################################# [100%]
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:systemd-udev-252-51.0.4.el9_6.1       ################################# [ 50%]
Cleaning up / removing...
   2:systemd-udev-252-51.0.4.el9_6.1       ################################# [100%]
```

Then telnet again, does not work: 
[root@aaaa zkarman]# telnet 1.1.11 9121
Trying 1.1.1.1...


Solution:
`podman network reload cache-exporter`  or `systemctl restart container-cache-exporter`

Please provide the full information as requested, i.e. `podman info`, you have omitted important information such as the netavark version.

Inn general this sounds like systemd is likely reseting/reloading the sysctl values we set on the interfaces. IF that is the case I would assume https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/1245 should fix that already so please test with netavark 1.16 or newer.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

Testing with netavark 1.16 or newer, then back to you. Thanks.

Sorry does not resolved.

```
[root@test-1 user]# podman info | grep avar
  networkBackend: netavark
    backend: netavark
    package: netavark-1.16.0-1.el9.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark
    version: netavark 1.16.0
```

Do you just update packages one by one? These combinations do not get tested with mixed versions, as noted in the PR it requires at least a container-selinux update as well if you have selinux enabled https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/380


In any case please just compare the sysctl values before and after the update to see what gets chnaged and what we put in /run/sysctl.d to see if we do cover that one or not.

Hi the values are the same before and after.
```
[root@test]# cat /run/sysctl.d/10-netavark-podman0.conf
# autogenerated by netavark
net/ipv4/ip_forward = 1
net/ipv4/conf/podman0/route_localnet = 1
net/ipv4/conf/podman0/rp_filter = 2
```
And what are the actual sysctl values on the interface? Did you check the selinux contexts/audit.log?

This is changed to net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0 from 1, other 2 are the same.
If I run this command then works fine:
 sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1

Hi, I would summarise this issue.
If your /etc/sysctl.conf is contains net.ipv4.ip_forward=0, required replicate into  net.ipv4.ip_forward=1.
Then works fine.
You can close this ticket.
Thanks for your help to find the root cause.
